CREATE TABLE logarithm_test (b FLOAT, x FLOAT);
SELECT log(b, x) FROM logarithm_test WHERE x > 0;
SELECT log(b, x) AS result FROM logarithm_test WHERE b > 1 AND x > b;
SELECT b, x, log(b, x) AS logarithm FROM logarithm_test WHERE x IS NOT NULL;
SELECT (SELECT log(b, x) FROM logarithm_test WHERE b = 2) AS log_base2, (SELECT log(b, x) FROM logarithm_test WHERE b = 10 AND x = 100.0) AS log_base10;
CREATE TABLE additional_data (b FLOAT, y FLOAT);
SELECT log(a.b, a.x) FROM logarithm_test a JOIN additional_data b ON a.b = b.b WHERE a.x = 64.0;
SELECT AVG(log(b, x)) FROM logarithm_test WHERE b > 1 AND x < 150;
WITH temp AS (SELECT log(b, x) AS log_value FROM logarithm_test WHERE x >= 1) SELECT SUM(log_value) FROM temp;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM logarithm_test WHERE log(b, x) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT MAX(log(b, x)) AS max_log FROM logarithm_test WHERE x > 0 GROUP BY b;
